inputs.prompt: Story: New research from a university urban planning department shows that cities implementing 15-minute neighbourhood policies saw a 22% reduction in car trips within 3 years — the largest study of its kind, covering 8 cities. Journalist: Lisa Chen, transport correspondent, The National Post. Recent article: She wrote about congestion pricing last month. Offer: Exclusive interview with the lead researcher before the study is published next week. Spokesperson: Prof. David Okafor, University of Westfield. My name: Tom Reid, Communications Manager, University of Westfield.

Story: A local bakery has been named in the top 50 bakeries in the country by a food magazine. Journalist: Food editor at the regional newspaper. Offer: Interview with the owner. My name: Anna Smith, bakery owner.

Story: Nordic Health Initiative has published the first cross-border study comparing cancer treatment outcomes across all five Nordic countries, revealing significant disparities despite similar healthcare systems. The study covers 200,000 patient records over 10 years. Journalist: Hans Eriksen, health correspondent, Politiken (Denmark). Recent article: He covered Nordic healthcare staffing shortages last quarter. Offer: Embargoed briefing with the study lead 48 hours before publication, plus access to the full dataset. Spokesperson: Dr. Ingrid Nørgaard, Executive Director. My name: Katrine Holm, Head of Communications, Nordic Health Initiative.
